provenance.source_corpus: Effects of Changes in Acidity on Aquatic Insects in Rocky Littoral Habitats of Lakes Near Sudbur y, Ontario

source_spans.text: Neutralization led to recolonization or increased abundance of the acid-sensitive mayfly, Stenacron interpunctatum
Neutralization led to recolonization or increased abundance of the acid-sensitive mayfly, Stenacron interpunctatum
Neutralization led to recolonization or increased abundance of the acid-sensitive mayfly, Stenacron interpunctatum, and the dragonfly, Boyeria grafiana
Direct and indirect effects of lake acidity appeared to be major controls on the structure of these communities
Declines in abundances of several taxa of Ephemeroptera at pH below 5.5 were attributable to acid toxicity
increases in the abundances of Odonata and Diptera at pH below 5.5 were associated with the absence of fish predators
The decrease in abundance of dragonflies (A. eremita, A. interrupta, and L. glacialis) in Whirligig Lake after neutralization
with concurrent increased predation pressure from introduced fish
